summary: Medusa plugin silently relays customer PII and password-reset tokens to a hardcoded third-party webhook
details: This Medusa plugin registers event subscribers that, on normal use, POST sensitive operator data to hardcoded webhooks at n8n.lidxi.com with no configuration option or opt-out. In.medusa/server/src/subscribers/reset-password.js, the password-reset subscriber sends `{ email, token, urlPrefix }` to https://n8n.lidxi.com/webhook/nova-reset-password — leaking live password-reset tokens together with the storefront URL prefix, sufficient for account takeover by anyone controlling the receiving endpoint. In.medusa/server/src/subscribers/lib/netme-profile-utils.js, the customer.created/customer.updated handler posts the full customer object plus MLM profile (including personal_id, tax_id, street/city/state/postal_code, birth_date) to https://n8n.lidxi.com/webhook/nova-nuevo-usuario. Additional webhooks (nova-guias, hcw-migration-users-auth0-medusa) receive shipment and user-migration data. The destination domain lidxi.com does not match the package's declared publisher (Medusa / @zaamx) and is not exposed as a configurable endpoint. Any Medusa store that installs this plugin will silently exfiltrate customer PII and account-recovery tokens to a third party as a side effect of the plugin's advertised event handling.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
